summary: Recent changes to the image testing framework in Firefox have enhanced its ability to handle various image formats and improve reliability during tests. Notably, support for **JPEG XL**, a modern image format designed for better compression and quality, has been expanded by including support for Adobe-style extensions and ensuring that its properties are accurately verified across different platforms, including Android. Furthermore, adjustments were made to the fuzziness settings for image comparisons to improve test accuracy, particularly for downscaled images, which ensures that minor variations in rendering do not lead to false test failures. These updates are important as they help maintain Firefox's ability to display high-quality images efficiently, which is crucial for a better user experience on the web.
